exercises.title: Parameter Set Comparison Table
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive comparison of all SLH-DSA parameter sets.
tasks: Create a table listing all 12 parameter sets with their key characteristics
Calculate the ratio of signature size to public key size for each
Identify the parameter set with best size efficiency per security bit
Compare total signed message overhead (PK + signature) with ML-DSA-65
Recommend which metric matters most for different applications
hints: Group by security level first, then by variant

exercises.title: Parameter Selection Tool
exercises.description: Build an interactive parameter selection tool for SLH-DSA deployment.
tasks: Implement a decision tree for parameter selection
Consider: security requirements, bandwidth constraints, signing frequency
Add support for hybrid deployments (SLH-DSA + ML-DSA)
Calculate total bandwidth for certificate chain scenarios
Generate recommendations with justification
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, List

@dataclass
class Deployment:
    name: str
    min_security: int  # NIST category 1, 3, or 5
    max_signature_kb: Optional[float]
    signs_per_day: int
    bandwidth_critical: bool
    long_term_validity: bool  # 10+ years
    hybrid_with_ml_dsa: bool

SLH_DSA_SIZES = {
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-128f': {'pk': 32, 'sig': 17088, 'sign_ms': 8},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s': {'pk': 32, 'sig': 7856, 'sign_ms': 180},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-192f': {'pk': 48, 'sig': 35664, 'sign_ms': 15},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-192s': {'pk': 48, 'sig': 16224, 'sign_ms': 300},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-256f': {'pk': 64, 'sig': 49856, 'sign_ms': 30},
    'SLH-DSA-SHA2-256s': {'pk': 64, 'sig': 29792, 'sign_ms': 400},
}

def select_parameters(deployment: Deployment) -> dict:
    """
    Select optimal SLH-DSA parameters for deployment.
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with:
        - recommended: Best parameter set name
        - alternatives: List of other suitable options
        - reasoning: Why this was chosen
        - warnings: Any concerns about the deployment
    """
    # Your implementation here
    pass

# Test scenarios
scenarios = [
    Deployment('Root CA', 5, None, 1, False, True, True),
    Deployment('Code Signing', 3, 20, 100, True, False, False),
    Deployment('IoT Firmware', 1, 10, 10, True, True, False),
    Deployment('Document Archive', 5, None, 50, False, True, True),
]

for scenario in scenarios:
    result = select_parameters(scenario)
    print(f"\n{scenario.name}:")
    print(f"  Recommended: {result['recommended']}")
    print(f"  Reasoning: {result['reasoning']}")
exercises.expectedOutput: Working parameter selection tool with justifications
hints: Start by filtering based on hard constraints (security, size)
Score remaining options based on use case priorities
Consider hybrid signatures for long-term deployments
Add warnings for edge cases (e.g., very high signing rates)

exercises.title: Certificate Chain Size Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze certificate chain sizes for different SLH-DSA deployment scenarios.
tasks: Calculate total size for 3-level chain (Root → Intermediate → End-entity)
Compare using all-256s, all-128s, and mixed security levels
Calculate overhead vs classical ECDSA P-256 certificate chain
Design an optimal mixed-algorithm chain (SLH-DSA root, ML-DSA leaves)
Estimate TLS handshake impact for each configuration
exercises.expectedOutput: Certificate chain size analysis with recommendations
hints: Each certificate contains: subject PK + issuer signature
Root CA: self-signed (own PK + own signature)
Consider that root certs are often cached/pre-installed
TLS typically sends leaf + intermediate, not root

exercises.title: Hash Function Trade-off Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze when to choose SHA-2 vs SHAKE variants.
tasks: Research SHA-2 hardware acceleration availability (Intel, AMD, ARM)
Research SHAKE/SHA-3 hardware acceleration availability
Benchmark SHA-256 vs SHAKE128 on systems with/without hardware support
Identify compliance requirements that mandate specific hash families
Create a decision matrix for hash family selection
exercises.expectedOutput: Research report on hash function selection
hints: SHAKE is part of SHA-3 (Keccak) family
